adult.label: Cycle Syncing 2026
adult.desc: Match your life to your hormones

emergency.label: Always Prepared ⚡
emergency.desc: Never get caught off guard again

gifter.label: Gift the Glow-Up 💝
gifter.desc: What she actually wants this cycle

holistic.label: Zero Waste Cycle 🌱
holistic.desc: Ditch disposables — your body will thank you

teen.text: It takes 2–3 years after your first period for your cycle to fully regulate. You're not broken — you're calibrating.
Your brain literally changes throughout your cycle. In the days before your period, creative thinking actually peaks. No cap.
Estrogen affects serotonin. Low estrogen before your period means lower serotonin — the emotional sensitivity is real, not dramatic.
'Period brain' is real. Iron loss during your period affects focus and memory. Iron-rich foods and rest genuinely help.
Cramps are caused by prostaglandins — the same chemicals that cause inflammation. Heat + ibuprofen work together for a reason.
Your skin, energy, mood, digestion, and sleep all change throughout your cycle. Understanding the pattern is literally a superpower.

adult.text: Your luteal phase metabolism rises by ~5–10%. Those carb cravings before your period are your body asking for more fuel. Biochemistry, not weakness.
Estrogen affects serotonin. Progesterone affects GABA. Your emotional map is hormonal — and it's always valid.
1 in 10 women has endometriosis. Average time to diagnosis: 7–10 years. Advocating for your pain is always the right move.
Your pain tolerance actually drops in the luteal phase — up to 35% in some studies. You're not weak. You're hormonally accurate.
PCOS affects up to 10% of women and is a leading cause of infertility. Many cases go undiagnosed for years. Know your cycle, know your body.
Magnesium deficiency is common in the luteal phase and linked directly to PMS severity. Dark chocolate + magnesium glycinate: not a coincidence.

holistic.text: Conventional pads and tampons may contain trace pesticides and synthetic fragrances. Organic cotton alternatives avoid these exposures entirely.
The average person uses 10,000–16,000 disposable period products in their lifetime. Reusables like discs and cloth pads eliminate this waste entirely.
CBD and magnesium work on the same prostaglandin pathways that cause cramps — both are evidence-backed for menstrual pain relief without pharmaceuticals.
Castor oil packs applied with heat have been used for centuries to ease uterine cramping and reduce pelvic inflammation. Science is beginning to validate why.
Red raspberry leaf has been shown to tone uterine muscles and reduce cramping duration. Cramp bark relaxes smooth muscle. Used together, they're powerful.
Clary sage essential oil contains sclareol — a phytoestrogen that may help balance estrogen during the luteal phase. Applied topically or diffused.

emergency.text: Always keep a backup. Setting up auto-delivery means you'll always have essentials — no more unexpected emergencies.
Stress (like an unexpected period) spikes cortisol, which can actually worsen cramps. Having a plan literally reduces pain.

gifter.text: Research shows that receiving care packages during a menstrual cycle significantly reduces reported emotional distress. Showing up matters.
Dark chocolate contains magnesium — a mineral that's often deficient during the luteal phase and directly linked to PMS. That's why it's craved.
